subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Ingredient Books
- **2** yellow ingredient books
- **2** green ingredient books
- **2** red ingredient books
- **2** blue ingredient books
- **2** purple ingredient books
- **1** orange ingredient book
- **1** black ingredient book

#### Cards & References
- **24** Fortune Teller cards
- **1** Almanac of Ingredients

#### Boards, Track, and Markers
- **4** pots (player boards)
- **1** scoring track with turn indicator
- **1** flame turn marker
- **4** scoring markers
- **4** rat stones
- **8** droplets (4 for game variation)
- **4** 0/50 seal tiles

#### Chips, Tokens, and Tools
- **215** ingredient chips
- **3** replacement chips
- **20** rubies
- **1** bonus die
- **4** bags
- **4** flasks

#### Starting Chips Per Player
- **4x white-1** chips
- **2x white-2** chips
- **1x white-3** chip
- **1x orange-1** chip
- **1x green-1** chip

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Place track** — Put the scoring track in the center of the table.
2. **Set round marker** — Put the flame on turn space 1.
3. **Set seal tiles** — Put all 4 seal tiles on matching spaces with 0 side up.
4. **Always-on books** — Lay out the orange and black ingredient books.
5. **Choose ingredient set** — Pick one set (1-4) for green/blue/red/yellow/purple books.
6. **First-round visible books** — Place only green, blue, and red books from the chosen set.
7. **Hold back books** — Keep yellow and purple books aside for later turns.
8. **Sort chips** — Group ingredient chips by color near their matching books.
9. **Prepare card deck** — Shuffle Fortune Teller cards and place face down near start player.

#### Turn-Based Book Unlocks
- **Turn 2 unlock** — Reveal yellow ingredient book.
- **Turn 3 unlock** — Reveal purple ingredient book.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player Takes
- **1 pot** in chosen color
- **1 bag** in chosen color
- **1 flask** in chosen color
- **1 scoring marker** in chosen color
- **1 rat stone** in chosen color

#### Place Player Pieces
- **Score marker** — Place on matching colored seal tile.
- **Pot side** — Use side without test tubes face up.
- **Droplet** — Place on space 0 in the pot center.
- **Flask** — Place on large trivet with full side up.
- **Rat stone** — Place on small trivet left of flask.

#### Fill Starting Bag
- **Add 9 chips** — 4x white-1, 2x white-2, 1x white-3, 1x orange-1, 1x green-1.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **Start player** — Last person who cooked goes first and holds the Fortune Teller deck.
- **Game length** — Play exactly 9 turns.

#### Fortune Teller Timing
- **Reveal step** — At start of each turn, reveal and read the top Fortune Teller card.
- **Purple card timing** — Resolve immediately before the turn starts.
- **Blue card timing** — Effect lasts through turn or resolves at end, as written.

#### Book Timing Reminder
- **Yellow book** — Becomes active on turn 2.
- **Purple book** — Becomes active on turn 3.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Reveal card** — Reveal and resolve the Fortune Teller card.
2. **Rats phase** — From turn 2 onward, trailing players move rat stones by rat-tail count.
3. **Brew phase** — All players draw and place chips simultaneously, one at a time.
4. **Explosion check** — Any player with white-chip total above 7 explodes and must stop.
5. **Stop condition** — End brew when all players either stop voluntarily or explode.
6. **Evaluation A-F** — Resolve bonus die, end-phase chip effects, rubies, VP/coins choices, and refill effects in order.
7. **Cleanup** — Return drawn chips to bags and advance turn marker.

#### Evaluation Order (A-F)
- **A Bonus Die** — Highest non-exploded scoring space rolls (all tied highest roll).
- **B End-Phase Chip Actions** — Resolve black/green/purple effects by current ingredient books.
- **C Rubies** — Any player on a ruby space gains 1 ruby.
- **D Victory Points** — Gain VP from your scoring space.
- **E Buy Chips** — Spend coins from scoring space to buy ingredients.
- **F Refill/Advance Effects** — Refill flask and resolve droplet advancement effects where allowed.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Brewing Actions
- **Draw blind** — Draw 1 chip from your bag without looking inside.
- **First placement** — Place first chip after droplet, or after rat stone if used this turn.
- **Later placement** — Place each next chip forward by its printed value (1/2/3/4).
- **Gaps stay empty** — Do not fill skipped spaces.
- **Push-or-stop choice** — After each draw, choose to stop or keep drawing.
- **Empty bag rule** — If bag is empty, you must stop.

#### Explosion Rules
- **Count whites only** — Only white chip values count toward explosion.
- **Explosion threshold** — If white total exceeds 7, potion explodes immediately.
- **Last chip stays** — The exploding chip is still placed in the pot.
- **Effects still apply** — Explosion does not cancel valid Fortune Teller or chip effects.

#### Chip Effect Timing
- **No power chips** — White and orange chips have no extra effect.
- **Immediate chips** — Blue, red, and yellow resolve when drawn.
- **End-phase chips** — Black, green, and purple resolve during evaluation.
- **Optional effects** — You may decline optional chip actions when allowed.

#### Flask Use
- **Valid use** — If your last drawn chip is white, you may return it to your bag by using a full flask.
- **Mark spent flask** — Flip flask to empty side after use.
- **Continue drawing** — You may keep drawing after flask use.
- **Explosion restriction** — You cannot use flask if that last white chip caused the explosion.
- **Use limit** — Flask is normally usable once per turn until refilled.

#### Evaluation Choices
- **No-explosion benefit** — Non-exploded players can use normal evaluation benefits, including bonus die eligibility.
- **Explosion penalty** — Exploded players skip bonus die and must choose VP or buy, not both.

#### Buying Chips
- **Coin source** — Spend coins shown on your scoring space.
- **Standard cap** — Buy up to 2 chips in a turn.
- **Color rule** — Bought chips must be different colors unless an effect says otherwise.
- **Bag timing** — New chips go into your bag for future turns.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Rat Catch-Up System
- **Activation timing** — Rats start on turn 2.
- **Catch-up amount** — Count rat tails between leader and your score marker.
- **Temporary boost** — Move rat stone ahead of droplet by that amount for this round only.
- **No permanent move** — Rat stone does not permanently advance your droplet.

#### Ingredient Set System
- **Set variability** — Green/blue/red/yellow/purple each have Set 1-4 rule variations.
- **Always active books** — Orange and black books are used every game.
- **Rule source** — Use book text and Almanac for exact chip effects.

#### Bonus Die System
- **Who rolls** — Highest non-exploded scoring space rolls; ties all roll.
- **Possible rewards** — Gain VP, gain rubies, advance droplet, or gain an orange chip.

#### Scoring Space Dual Value
- **Two outputs** — Scoring space grants both VP and coin value.
- **Explosion limit** — Exploded players must choose only one of those two outputs.
- **Ruby spaces** — Ruby icons pay out regardless of explosion status.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
- **End trigger** — Game ends after turn 9 evaluation and cleanup.

#### Final Scoring Sources
- **Track points** — Count all victory points earned on the scoring track during play.
- **Ruby conversion** — Convert remaining rubies to VP at the rulebook rate.
- **Coin conversion** — Convert leftover money/coins to VP at the rulebook rate.
- **Final chip effects** — Add any endgame VP from ingredient effects in your active set.

#### Winner & Tiebreakers
- **Primary winner** — Highest total VP wins.
- **Tiebreak 1** — If tied, farther position on scoring track wins.
- **Tiebreak 2** — If still tied, more rubies wins.
- **Final tie** — If still tied, share victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Bag Control
- **Stabilize first** — Use first purchases to reduce white-risk density before chasing huge turns.
- **Reliable tempo** — A safe turn 1-3 curve usually beats one lucky explosion-prone spike.

#### Flask Discipline
- **Save for danger** — Keep flask for a late white draw near explosion threshold.
- **Avoid waste** — Don’t spend flask on harmless early whites unless set effects justify it.

#### Rat-Tail Planning
- **Use catch-up turns** — If slightly behind, leverage rat stones to reach better coin spaces safely.
- **Convert tempo to engine** — Spend those boosted turns on chips that improve future rounds.

#### Set-Specific Buying
- **Buy for synergy** — Prioritize chips that trigger strong effects in your current ingredient set.
- **Ignore sticker price trap** — Cheaper synergy chips often outperform expensive unsynergized chips.

#### Turn-2/Turn-3 Unlock Prep
- **Hold coin flexibility** — Keep enough buying power to exploit yellow and purple books when they unlock.
- **Pre-plan pivots** — Decide before turn 2 which color package you’ll pivot into.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Over-pushing every round** — Constant greed causes repeated explosions and weak long-term bag growth.
- **Ignoring draw math** — Evaluating only the next chip instead of remaining white totals leads to avoidable busts.
- **Overbuying expensive chips early** — Big costs can delay critical mid-cost stabilizers and slow your engine.
- **Forgetting timing windows** — Confusing draw-time vs evaluation-time chip effects loses value and causes rules errors.
- **Illegal flask take-back** — You cannot flask a white chip that caused your explosion; enforce this strictly.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Stop Now vs Draw One More
- **Stop now** — Lock in your current VP/coins and avoid explosion penalties.
- **Draw one more** — Chase higher rewards at increased bust risk.

#### Decision 2: VP Now vs Economy Now
- **Take VP** — Keep scoreboard pressure, especially when already ahead.
- **Take coins** — Improve future bag quality, often yielding higher total over 9 turns.

#### Decision 3: Broad Mix vs Focused Synergy
- **Broad mix** — Increases consistency and reduces dependence on perfect draws.
- **Focused synergy** — Creates stronger spike turns when your key colors cluster.

#### Decision 4: Safe Round vs Catch-Up Round
- **Safe round** — Stabilize when your bag is white-heavy.
- **Catch-up round** — Push harder when rat-tail boost and flask are both available.

metadata.notes: Primary content based on provided Quacks rulebook excerpt; ingredient-set specifics reference the Almanac/book system called out in the rules.
